message:
fcp-store symbol_store: scrub corrupt symbols on read paths

Defend against symbols whose data size does not match the declared
OTI symbol_size in ObjectSymbolMeta. Such symbols can arrive from
partial writes, torn reads on a crashing peer, or a bug in a remote
node's erasure encoder — and they cause reconstruction to fail in
confusing ways (symbol_count > K but decode errors).

Approach:

- Add `symbol_matches_meta`: a StoredSymbol is kept iff its object_id,
  zone_id, and data.len() all agree with the object's SymbolMeta /
  OTI.symbol_size.
- Add `scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked`: retains only matching symbols,
  returns bytes removed so we can keep `used_bytes` consistent.
- Upgrade the read locks on get_symbol, get_all_symbols, symbol_count,
  get_distribution, and can_reconstruct to WRITE locks so they can
  scrub before returning. After scrubbing, drop the write lock and
  update `used_bytes` under its own lock (no lock ordering change).

This is opportunistic self-healing: we don't schedule a scan, we just
fix things as readers observe them. The trade-off is that read paths
now briefly take the write lock — acceptable because (a) the scrub
is O(symbols-for-one-object) and runs only on touched objects, and
(b) correctness beats lock contention for these paths.

New test: scrub_corrupt_symbol_before_reconstruct_and_distribution.
Inserts a 63-byte symbol under an OTI that specifies a different
symbol_size, then verifies every read path reports the object as
having zero symbols (not "K symbols but undecodable"), that
storage_used drops to 0, and that get_symbol returns NotFound (not
a silently-bad symbol). Logs `scrubbed_corrupt_symbol: true` to the
StoreLogData trail so the event is greppable in test output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
